title: Ericoides demissum
taxonomy: Ericoides demissum is classified as a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Gentianaceae. The genus Ericoides is a small one and Ericoides demissum is its only known species. The plant is native to the Himalayas and is often found in high-altitude regions. It is also known as the Himalayan gentian.

constituents: Ericoides demissum contains several active constituents, including triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties and have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's saponins have been shown to exhibit cytotoxic activity against certain cancer cell lines. Flavonoids present in Ericoides demissum have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is necessary to fully understand the extent of its active constituents and their effects on human health.
parts: Ericoides demissum, also known as the lesser dodder, has been found to have medicinal properties in various cultures. The plant is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The leaves, stems, and roots of Ericoides demissum are used to prepare medicinal extracts. The plant contains bioactive compounds that are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Ericoides demissum.
intro: Ericoides demissum, also known as Winter's Bark, is a plant species within the family Apiaceae, traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Winter's Bark include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ericoides demissum include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Winter's Bark include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory infections.
